pairs.question: What is Nerd Fonts and what is its primary purpose?
pairs.answer: Nerd Fonts is a project that patches popular programming fonts with additional glyphs (icons) from iconic font collections like Font Awesome, Material Design Icons, Octicons, and more. Its primary purpose is to enhance developer-targeted fonts with a high number of extra glyphs to support a richer display of icons in terminal applications and other developer tools.

pairs.question: How many glyphs and patched fonts does Nerd Fonts offer?
pairs.answer: Nerd Fonts includes over 3,600 icons and patches more than 50 font families, including popular ones like Hack and Source Code Pro.

pairs.question: What are the two main methods to install Nerd Fonts on macOS?
pairs.answer: On macOS, you can install Nerd Fonts using Homebrew (Option 2) or by downloading release archives (Option 1).

pairs.question: Provide a PowerShell command snippet to install Nerd Fonts interactively on Windows.
pairs.answer:
You can use the following PowerShell command to install Nerd Fonts interactively:
powershell
# Run this in an elevated PowerShell session
Set-ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Scope Process -Force; [System.Net.ServicePointManager]::SecurityProtocol = [System.Net.ServicePointManager]::SecurityProtocol -bor 3072; iex ((New-Object System.Net.WebClient).DownloadString('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ryanoasis/nerd-fonts/master/install.ps1'))

pairs.question: What are the three glyph width options available when patching a font with Nerd Fonts?
pairs.answer: When using the Nerd Fonts Font Patcher, you can choose between three glyph width options: Monospaced (fixed-width), double-width (non-monospaced), or proportional.

pairs.question: How can you search for specific glyphs in Nerd Fonts?
pairs.answer: You can search for specific glyphs on the Nerd Fonts website using the Cheat Sheet at [NerdFonts.com](https://www.nerdfonts.com/cheat-sheet). This tool allows you to look up icons by name and see their corresponding Unicode code points.

pairs.question: What are the two recommended methods for installing Nerd Fonts if you want automation or script integration?
pairs.answer: For automation or script integration, you can use either the Install Script (Option 7) or the Ad Hoc Curl Download (Option 6). The Install Script allows for automated installation, while the curl command can be integrated into scripts for downloading fonts directly.
